title: Siargao Boat Rental Costs: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
When you rent a boat in Siargao, you can explore the Pacific from General Luna for around €60 to €250 per day. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Siargao or list your own, making it easy to compare skippered charters in Siargao and other options.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Siargao?

Boat rental in Siargao starts at €60 for a shared tour and goes up to €500 for a crewed yacht. Budget-friendly outrigger boats, known as bangkas, are the most affordable option. A half-day private charter for up to six guests typically costs between €80 and €150. For a full-day catamaran, expect to pay €200 to €400. Premium yachts with crew can exceed €500, especially during peak season. Compare prices and availability on skippered charters in Siargao.

Where to set off in Siargao

Most boat trips depart from General Luna, the main tourist hub on the island. The public port at Dapa is another key departure point, offering access to the nearby islands. From there, you can head to Daku Island, Guyam Island, and Naked Island, all a short ride away. The sheltered waters of Del Carmen Bay provide calmer conditions for beginners. Further north, the port at Santa Monica gives access to the Pacific surf breaks. Hire a boat from any of these locations to suit your itinerary.

Which boat to rent

  1. Bangka (outrigger canoe), ideal for island hopping, up to 6 guests, from €80 for half a day.
  2. Motorboat, best for fishing trips or touring nearby islands, up to 8 guests, from €150 for half a day.
  3. Catamaran, stable and spacious for day trips, up to 12 guests, from €250 for a full day.
  4. Crewed yacht, for a luxury experience with a skipper and host, up to 10 guests, from €400 for a full day. For a real example, see the Salty Sun Charters charter page.

Cost breakdown by boat type

  • Shared island-hopping tour: €60 per person, includes lunch and snorkelling stops.
  • Small motorboat (half-day, up to 6 guests): €80, €120 for the entire boat.
  • Catamaran (full-day, up to 12 guests): €250, €350.
  • 44-foot crewed yacht with Azimut or Sunseeker build: €450, €500 per day, including fuel and crew.

When to go and do you need a licence?

Peak season runs from March to May, when the Amihan wind brings calm seas and clear skies. The low season from June to October can still offer good conditions but with more rain. For boat rental in Siargao, you do not need a licence if you hire a skippered boat. For bareboat hire, a recognised licence may be required; always check with the operator. The operator confirms exact requirements before booking.

During peak season, booking a boat at least two weeks in advance is wise because demand is high and many vessels get reserved early.

At a glance

  • Price band: €60, €500 per day
  • Peak months: March, April, May
  • Typical trip length: half-day or full-day
  • Typical guest capacity: 6, 12
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Siargao

Can I rent a boat for just a few hours?

Yes, many operators offer half-day charters lasting around four hours, which is enough to visit two or three nearby islands.

Is it safe to rent a boat in Siargao?

All boats listed on BoatHire24 are with licensed operators who follow safety regulations, and skippered charters include a local captain experienced in the local waters.

What is the best time of day for a boat trip?

Morning departures around 8am are popular to avoid afternoon heat and to see the best light for photography.

Can I bring my own food and drink on board?

Most rented boats allow you to bring your own provisions, but some operators include lunch and refreshments in the price.

Whether you are planning a day trip to the nearby islets or a longer voyage along the coast, BoatHire24 helps you find the right boat at the right price. If you own a boat in Siargao, you can list your vessel on the BoatHire24 marketplace and start earning extra income from the growing tourism demand in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Siargao and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
